summary: High‑energy show with soaring Brent, stellar China Doll & powerful Cassidy
blurb: The first set rockets forward with Hey Pocky Way, Jack Straw and a ripping Detroit Medley, while the second half delivers China Doll, The Greatest Story Ever Told > Devil With The Blue Dress > Good Golly Miss Molly > Devil With The Blue Dress and an explosive Mighty Quinn encore. Brent Mydland’s keyboards dominate, and Jerry Garcia’s lead shines on China Doll. A must‑listen for fans of the Dead’s late‑80s firepower.
review: Providence, RI — September 9, 1987 captured one of the Grateful Dead’s most relentless evenings of that era. The band kicked off with a punchy Hey Pocky Way and a tight Jack Straw before launching into an unforgettable Detroit Medley that set the tone for a night of nonstop momentum. Highlights poured out of Brent Mydland’s arsenal – his fireworks on The Greatest Story Ever Told > Devil With The Blue Dress > Good Golly Miss Molly > Devil With The Blue Dress are often described as “pure Brent greatness,” and his soaring keyboards carried the second set through a transcendent China Doll, where Jerry’s lead guitar was called “near‑religious.” Even the encore hit hard with an electrifying Mighty Quinn that left the hall buzzing. While a few moments – notably The Wheel and some mid‑set jams – were seen as less inspired, the overall pacing stayed high, making this show a standout snapshot of the band’s late‑80s vigor.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across all analyses the sentiment is overwhelmingly positive: multiple recordings praise Brent’s keyboard fireworks, Jerry’s soaring leads on China Doll and Jack Straw, and the high‑energy flow of the setlist. No consistent negative comments about performance quality appear; only isolated “mixed” notes refer to specific song choices, not the playing itself. This strong, repeated acclaim justifies a substantial positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard capture with crisp overall mix, highlights the high‑energy set and Brent’s keyboard fireworks while avoiding the tinny early quirks of other sources.

key_highlights: Brent's fireworks on The Greatest Story Ever Told > Devil With The Blue Dress > Good Golly Miss Molly > Devil With The Blue Dress
Jerry’s soaring lead on China Doll and sharp solo on Jack Straw
Relentless first‑set energy with Hey Pocky Way, Jack Straw and Detroit Medley
Explosive Mighty Quinn encore that caps the night

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers soaring, near‑religious lead on China Doll and a sharp, trippy solo on Jack Straw; his playing is consistently described as ‘on fire’ throughout the night.
band_performance.Phil: Drops a “bomb” during the final jam of Jack Straw, driving the band into high‑octane stretch; otherwise low‑key in reviews.
band_performance.Brent: Pure Brent greatness – keyboards are described as soaring, exploding and the primary driver of the show, especially on The Greatest Story sequence and throughout the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides a solid rhythmic foundation; no specific critiques but noted as part of a strong overall drum section.
